data: intermediate-concept-connections
Intermediate Concept Connections
Intermediate / Fundamentals
Connect learning, reasoning, and evidence concepts across slightly deeper examples.
icc-1
multiple-choice
A learner rereads a chapter twice but still cannot answer practice questions. Which concept best explains the gap?
Recognition can feel familiar without proving retrieval
Rereading always removes the need for practice
Practice questions only measure typing speed
Familiarity and mastery are identical
Think about the difference between seeing an answer and producing it.
Rereading can create familiarity, but retrieval practice checks whether the learner can recall and use the idea.
icc-2
fill-blank
The amount of mental effort a task places on working memory is cognitive _____.
load
The phrase describes how heavy the task feels mentally.
Cognitive load is the demand a task places on working memory.
icc-3
true-false
If two measures move together, that pattern alone proves that one caused the other.
A relationship can exist without proving direction or cause.
Correlation can suggest a relationship, but causal claims need stronger evidence and attention to alternative explanations.
icc-4
Why can comparing an example and a nonexample help someone learn a concept?
It highlights the defining feature that makes the concept apply
It guarantees every future problem has only one detail
It removes the need for explanations
It makes the concept depend on answer order
A nonexample is useful because it almost fits, but not quite.
Example and nonexample comparisons clarify the boundaries of a concept by showing what counts and what does not.
icc-5
A quiz objective says learners should 'compare two explanations,' but every question asks for definitions only. What is misaligned?
The assessment does not match the objective
The objective has too many words
Definitions can never be assessed
The quiz title is too short
Compare the skill named by the objective with the skill required by the questions.
Assessment questions should give learners a chance to demonstrate the skill named in the objective.
icc-6
Which schedule best combines spacing and retrieval?
Short practice attempts on three different days with answers hidden first
One long rereading session with all answers visible
A single review after the quiz ends
Only reviewing the easiest card repeatedly
The best option spreads practice and asks the learner to recall.
Short attempts across multiple days with hidden answers combine spaced practice and retrieval practice.
icc-7
A cycle where an attempt leads to information that improves the next attempt is a feedback _____.
loop
The process circles back into the next try.
A feedback loop uses results from one attempt to adjust the next attempt.
icc-8
A student sorts four scenarios by whether they include measurable evidence. Which thinking skill are they practicing?
Classification using a defining criterion
Memorizing answer positions
Avoiding comparison
Changing the topic
They are grouping examples according to a rule.
Classification applies a criterion to decide which items belong together.
icc-9
Transfer means using a concept in a new situation, not only repeating it in the original example.
The key idea is using learning somewhere else.
Transfer happens when learners apply knowledge or skills beyond the exact practice context.
icc-10
A lesson starts with a worked example, then asks learners to solve a similar problem with fewer hints. Why is this sequence useful?
It gradually shifts responsibility from the example to the learner
It prevents learners from ever practicing independently
It makes the explanation optional in every case
It hides the goal of the lesson
The support decreases as the learner gains footing.
Fading support helps learners move from guided understanding toward independent performance.
icc-11
Knowledge needed before learning a more complex idea is called _____ knowledge.
prerequisite
It comes before the new material.
Prerequisite knowledge is the foundation a learner needs before tackling a more advanced concept.
icc-12
A concept map is most useful when a learner needs to show what?
Relationships among ideas
The fastest way to click a button
A single memorized definition only
The number of letters in each answer
Concept maps connect nodes.
Concept maps help learners organize and explain relationships among ideas.
